title: Digitally Signed Emails Are More Secure Cyber Awareness
source_filename: Digitally Signed Emails Are More Secure Cyber Awareness.docx

qas.q: A vendor conducting a pilot program with your organization contacts you for organizational data to use in a prototype. How should you respond?
qas.a: Refer the vendor to the appropriate personnel.

qas.q: When classified data is not in use, how can you protect it?
qas.a: Store classified data appropriately in a GSA-approved vault/container.

qas.q: What is the basis for handling and storage of classified data?
qas.a: Classification markings and handling caveats.

qas.q: Which of the following must you do before using an unclassified laptop and peripherals in a collateral classified environment?
qas.a: Ensure that any cameras, microphones, and Wi-Fi embedded in the laptop are physically disabled.

qas.q: What level of damage to national security can you reasonably expect Top secret information to cause if disclosed?
qas.a: Exceptionally grave damage.

qas.q: Which of the following is true about telework?
qas.a: You must have your organization's permission to telework.

qas.q: Which of the following is true of protecting classified data?
qas.a: Classified material must be appropriately marked.

qas.q: Which of the following is a reportable insider threat activity?
qas.a: Attempting to access sensitive information without need-to-know.

qas.q: Which scenario might indicate a reportable insider threat?
qas.a: a colleague removes sensitive information without seeking authorization in order to perform authorized telework.

qas.q: Which of the following is a potential insider threat indicator?
qas.a: 1) Unusual interest in classified information. 2) Difficult life circumstances, such as death of spouse.

qas.q: Which piece of information is safest to include on your social media profile?
qas.a: Only use Government-approved equipment to process PII.

qas.q: Which of the following statements is true?
qas.a: Many apps and smart devices collect and share your personal information and contribute to your online identity.

qas.q: How can you protect your organization on social networking sites?
qas.a: Ensure there are no identifiable landmarks visible in any photos taken in a work setting that you post.

qas.q: Which is a best practice for protecting Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)?
qas.a: Store it in a locked desk drawer after working hours.

qas.q: Which of the following best describes a way to safely transmit Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)?
qas.a: Paul verifies that the information is CUI, includes a CUI marking in the subject header, and digitally signs an e-mail containing CUI.

qas.q: Which designation includes Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and Protected Health Information (PHI)?
qas.a: Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT an example of CUI?
qas.a: Press release data.

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT a correct way to protect CUI?
qas.a: CUI may be stored on any password-protected system.

qas.q: Which of the following best describes good physical security?
qas.a: Lionel stops an individual in his secure area who is not wearing a badge.

qas.q: Which of the following is an example of two-factor authentication?
qas.a: A Common Access Card and Personal Identification Number.

qas.q: What is the best way to protect your Common Access Card (CAC) or Personal Identity Verification (PIV) card?
qas.a: Store it in a shielded sleeve.

qas.q: What must authorized personnel do before permitting another individual to enter a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
qas.a: Confirm the individual's need-to-know and access.

qas.q: Which of the following is true of Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI)?
qas.a: Access requires Top Secret clearance and indoctrination into the SCI program.

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT a potential consequence of using removable media unsafely in a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
qas.a: Damage to the removable media.

qas.q: What portable electronic devices (PEDs) are permitted in a SCIF?
qas.a: Only expressly authorized government-owned PEDs.

qas.q: What is the response to an incident such as opening an uncontrolled DVD on a computer in a SCIF?
qas.a: All of these.

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT a type of malicious code?
qas.a: Executables.

qas.q: Which of the following actions can help tp protect your identity?
qas.a: Shred personal documents.

qas.q: Which is an appropriate use of government e-mail?
qas.a: Use a digital signature when sending attachments or hyperlinks.

qas.q: What type of social engineering targets particular groups of people?
qas.a: Spear phishing.

qas.q: How can you protect yourself from social engineering?
qas.a: Verify the identity of all individuals.

qas.q: Which of the following is true of traveling overseas with a mobile phone?
qas.a: A personally owned device approved under Bring Your Own Approved Device (BYOAD) policy must be unenrolled while out of the country.

qas.q: What should Sara do when using publicly available Internet, such as hotel Wi-Fi?
qas.a: Only connect with Government VPN.

qas.q: What is the danger of using public Wi-Fi connections?
qas.a: Both of these.

qas.q: Which of the following personally-owned computer peripherals is permitted for use with Government-furnished equipment?
qas.a: A headset with a microphone through a Universal Serial Bus (USB) port.

qas.q: How can you protect data on your mobile computing and portable electronic devices (PEDs)?
qas.a: Enable automatic screen locking after a period of inactivity.

qas.q: Which of the following is an example of removable media?
qas.a: External hard drive.

qas.q: Which of the following is true of Internet of Things (IoT) devices?
qas.a: They can become an attack vector to other devices on your home network.

qas.q: When is it appropriate to have your security badge visible?
qas.a: At all times when in the facility.

qas.q: What should the owner of this printed SCI do differently?
qas.a: Retrieve classified documents promptly from printers.

qas.q: What should the participants in this conversation involving SCI do differently?
qas.a: Physically assess that everyone within listening distance is cleared and has a need-to-know for the information being discussed.

qas.q: Which of the following demonstrates proper protection of mobile devices?
qas.a: Linda encrypts all of the sensitive data on her government-issued mobile devices.

qas.q: Which of the following does NOT constitute spillage?
qas.a: Classified information that should be unclassified and is downgraded.

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT an appropriate way to protect against inadvertent spillage?
qas.a: Use the classified network for all work, including unclassified work.

qas.q: Which of the following should you NOT do if you find classified information on the internet?
qas.a: Download the information.

qas.q: Who designates whether information is classified and its classification level?
qas.a: Follow procedures for transferring data to and from outside agency and non-government networks.

qas.q: Which of the following is a good practice to protect classified information?
qas.a: Original classification authority.

qas.q: Which of the following may help to prevent spillage?
qas.a: Avoid talking about work outside of the workplace or with people without a need-to-know.

qas.q: In addition to avoiding the temptation of greed to betray his country, what should Alex do differently?
qas.a: Report the suspicious behave in accordance with their organization's threat policy.

qas.q: How many insider threat indicators does Alex demonstrate?
qas.a: Digitally signed e-mails are more secure.

qas.q: What should Alex's colleagues do?
qas.a: It is often the default but can be prevented by disabling the location function.

qas.q: Which of the following is true?
qas.a: This is never okay.

qas.q: Which of the following best describes the conditions under which mobile devices and applications can track your location?
qas.a: Linda encrypts all of the sensitive data on her government-issued mobile devices.

qas.q: When is it okay to charge a personal mobile device using government-furnished equipment (GFE)?
qas.a: It may prohibit the use of a virtual private network (VPN).

qas.q: What security risk does a public Wi-Fi connection pose?
qas.a: If you participate in or condone it at any time.

qas.q: Which of the following represents an ethical use of your Government-furnished equipment (GFE)?
qas.a: Validate friend requests through another source through another source before confirming them.

qas.q: When may you be subject to criminal, disciplinary, and/or administrative action due to online harassment, bullying, stalking, hazing, discrimination, or retaliation?
qas.a: Photos of your pet.

qas.q: How can you protect yourself on social networking sites?
qas.a: They have similar features, and the same rules and protections apply to both.

qas.q: Which of the following is true of removable media and portable electronic devices (PEDs)?
qas.a: CUI must be handled using safeguarding or dissemination controls.

qas.q: Which of the following is a security best practice for protecting Personally Identifiable Information (PII)?
qas.a: CPCON 1.

qas.q: Which of the following is true of Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)?
qas.a: You should remove and take your CAC/PIV card whenever you leave your workstation.

qas.q: Which Cyber Protection Condition (CPCON) establishes a protection priority focus on critical functions only?
qas.a: %2ZN=Ugq

qas.q: Which of the following is true of the Common Access Card (CAC) or Personal Identity Verification (PIV) card?
qas.a: in any manner.

qas.q: Which of the following is an example of a strong password?
qas.a: Don't assume open storage in a secure facility is authorized.

qas.q: A compromise of Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI) occurs when a person who does not have the required clearance or access caveats comes into possession of SCI________.
qas.a: 2 indicators.

qas.q: Which of the following is a good practice to protest classified information?
qas.a: Adversaries exploit social networking sites to disseminate fake news.

qas.q: Based on the description that follows, how many potential insider threat indicators(s) are displayed? A colleague saves money for an overseas vacation every year, is a single father, and occasionally consumes alcohol.
qas.a: May be used to mask malicious intent.

qas.q: Which of the following is true about URLs?
qas.a: 1 indicator

qas.q: What does "spillage refer to?
qas.a: Research the source to evaluate its credibility and reliability.

qas.q: Based on the description that follows, haw many potential insider threat indicator(s) are displayed? a colleague enjoys playing videos games, regularly uses social media, and frequently forgets to secure her smartphone elsewhere before entering areas where it is prohibited.
qas.a: It contains certificates for identification, encryption, and digital signature.

qas.q: A trusted friend in your social network posts a link to vaccine information on a website unknown to you. What action should you take?
qas.a: only connect government-owned PEDs to the same level classification information system when authorized.

qas.q: Which of the following is true of the Common Access Card (CAC)?
qas.a: For Government-owned devices, use approved and authorized applications only.

qas.q: Which of the following is true of portable electronic devices (PEDs) in a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
qas.a: You should confirm that a site that wants to store a cookie uses an encrypted link.

qas.q: Which of the following is true of downloading apps?
qas.a: Search for instructions on how to preview where the link actually leads.

qas.q: Which of the following statements is true of cookies?
qas.a: Avoid inserting removable media with unknown content into your computer.

qas.q: What action should you take with a compressed Uniform Resource Locator (URL) on a website known to you?
qas.a: Implement Wi-Fi Protected Access 2 (WPA2) Personal encryption at a minimum.

qas.q: Which of the following is a best practice for using removable media?
qas.a: Whaling
